title: Question: performance improvement opportunity?
body:
---


Author Name: **Jie Xu** (@jiexu)
Original Redmine Message: 1311 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi All, 

I have run some profiling on Verilator compiled design, the major time is spent on some @comb_@ and @sequent_@ functions which I believe make lots of sense. However, in addition, you can find out there are several Verilator utility functions which take quite some time. My questions are 1) if these functions are really needed? 2) if they are necessary, can we optimize them to improve the model performance?

```
Each sample counts as 0.01 seconds.
  %   cumulative   self              self     total
 time   seconds   seconds    calls  us/call  us/call  name
 22.22      0.04     0.04                             Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__1074(Vsim_top__Syms*)
 16.67      0.07     0.03                             Vsim_top::_eval(Vsim_top__Syms*)
  8.33      0.09     0.02                             _ZL13VL_EXTENDS_QQiim
  5.56      0.11     0.01                             _ZL10VL_LTS_IIIiiijj
  5.56      0.12     0.01                             _ZL13VL_SHIFTL_WWIiiiPjPKjj
  5.56      0.13     0.01                             _ZL13VL_SHIFTR_WWIiiiPjPKjj
  5.56      0.14     0.01                             _ZL15VL_EXTENDSIGN_Iij
  5.56      0.15     0.01                             Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__230(Vsim_top__Syms*)
  5.56      0.16     0.01                             Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__526(Vsim_top__Syms*)
  5.56      0.17     0.01                             Vsim_top::_combo__TOP__1047(Vsim_top__Syms*)
  5.56      0.18     0.01                             Vsim_top::_sequent__TOP__289(Vsim_top__Syms*)
  2.78      0.18     0.01                             _ZL12VL_REDXOR_32j

```
